args.title: New SLP Referral – Emma Lee (DOB 2018-09-04) – Prior Auth Required

args.body:
Dear Daniel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy! We received the referral from Dr. Priya Nair at Northside Pediatrics for Emma's speech-language evaluation, and we're so glad you've chosen us to support her.

We have placed a tentative hold on the following appointment that matches your availability preference:

📅 Thursday, May 1, 2026 at 3:30 PM
👩‍⚕️ Provider: Maya Chen, MS CCC-SLP
📋 Type: Speech-Language Evaluation

Before we can confirm this appointment, our intake team needs to complete a couple of steps:
1. Gather Emma's intake paperwork.
2. Obtain prior authorization from Blue Cross Blue Shield (required for this evaluation).

A member of our team will be in touch within 1–2 business days to walk you through next steps and confirm the appointment.

In the meantime, if you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach us at our main office.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy Intake Team

items.recommended_next_action: Intake team must: (1) initiate prior authorization with BCBS before confirming the evaluation — auth is required per verified insurance data; (2) send intake paperwork to Daniel Lee; (3) confirm the tentative hold on Thursday May 1 at 3:30 PM with Maya Chen, MS CCC-SLP (hold ID: hold_01kskey4fy4vp9ka9w0kcvd0hm). Draft reply to parent is ready for review and send.

items.decision_rationale:
**Classification:** New referral — Emma Lee is not found in the patient database, and this is a fax referral from a pediatrician (Dr. Priya Nair, Northside Pediatrics) for a first-time SLP evaluation.

**Urgency: P2 (Standard)** — This is a routine new referral with no safeguarding concerns, no same-day scheduling urgency, and complete intake information. Standard 1–2 business day follow-up is appropriate.

**Insurance:** BCBS PPO was verified as ACTIVE and IN-NETWORK ($30 copay). No out-of-network benefits conversation is required. However, the system flagged that **prior authorization is required** — this is a critical blocker that must be resolved before the evaluation is confirmed.

**Service Line Check:** Emma is 7 years old (DOB 2018-09-04), well within the 0–18 age range. SLP is an offered discipline. Referral concern (articulation delay, reduced intelligibility) is appropriate for an SLP evaluation.

**Slot Selection:** The family explicitly requested after-school Tuesdays or Thursdays. The slot on Thursday, May 1 at 3:30 PM with Maya Chen, MS CCC-SLP directly matches this preference and was held (hold ID: hold_01kskey4fy4vp9ka9w0kcvd0hm). Other available slots (Wed Apr 30 at 11 AM, Mon May 4 at 2 PM, Wed Apr 29 at 9 AM) did not match the stated preference and were not held.

**Language:** No Spanish language preference indicated; English communication is appropriate.

**Tasks & Drafts:** An intake task (due 2026-04-28) was created covering prior auth initiation, paperwork, and slot confirmation. A parent-facing draft email was prepared — not auto-sent, pending staff review.

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Speech therapy evaluation requested; safeguarding concern — mother disclosed father has been 'getting rough with him during weekends'; child reportedly more clingy.

args.reason: Parent (Maria Gomez) disclosed during voicemail that the child Leo (age 6) has been experiencing the father "getting rough with him during weekends," which constitutes a potential disclosure of physical harm or unsafe caregiving. This requires immediate P0 safeguarding review by the clinical lead per clinic policy.

args.notes: URGENT – P0 Safeguarding Disclosure. Voicemail received 2026-04-27 at 19:43 PT from Maria Gomez (mother), phone: 555-0102. She disclosed that Leo's father has been "getting rough with him during weekends," and noted Leo has become more clingy. Leo is 6 years old and is not yet a patient (no record found in database). The clinical lead must review this disclosure immediately per safeguarding protocol and determine whether a mandatory report is required. Do NOT provide investigative advice to the family. A neutral acknowledgment message has been drafted for staff review before sending.
args.title: P0 Safeguarding Review – Leo Gomez (Age 6)

args.body: Hi Maria, thank you so much for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy. We received your voicemail about Leo and want you to know that we take every family's concerns very seriously. A member of our team will be in touch with you shortly. If you have any urgent concerns in the meantime, please don't hesitate to call us back at our main number. We look forward to connecting with you soon.

items.recommended_next_action: IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED: Clinical lead must review the safeguarding disclosure today (2026-04-27). Assess whether a mandatory report to child protective services is required per applicable state law and clinic protocol. Do NOT send any outbound message to the family until the clinical lead has reviewed and approved the neutral draft acknowledgment. Do not discuss the safeguarding concern in any outbound communication. The SLP evaluation request and scheduling inquiry should be addressed only after the safeguarding review is resolved.

escalation.reason: Parent (Maria Gomez) disclosed during voicemail that the child Leo (age 6) has been experiencing the father "getting rough with him during weekends," which constitutes a potential disclosure of physical harm or unsafe caregiving. This requires immediate P0 safeguarding review by the clinical lead per clinic policy.

items.decision_rationale:
**Classification: Safeguarding (P0)**

**Safeguarding Trigger:** The voicemail from Maria Gomez contains an explicit disclosure that Leo's father has been "getting rough with him during weekends," and that Leo has become more clingy as a result. Per Cedar Kids Therapy safeguarding policy, any disclosure suggesting harm, abuse, neglect, or unsafe caregiving must be treated as P0 and escalated to the clinical lead immediately. This language meets that threshold.

**Steps Taken:**
1. **search_patient:** Leo Gomez returned no existing record — he is a new patient.
2. **lookup_policy (safeguarding, scheduling, language_access):** Confirmed P0 protocol; neutral acknowledgment only; no investigative advice in outbound messages.
3. **escalate (P0):** Escalation filed immediately (esc_01kskez63ae3q41xtaptx98tnv) with full context of the disclosure.
4. **create_task (clinical_lead, same-day):** Task created for clinical lead to review the disclosure and determine mandatory reporting obligations (task_01kskez63cnsv5rscz79ypqa6f). Due today per P0 protocol.
5. **draft_message:** A neutral, empathetic acknowledgment drafted for staff review before sending — contains no investigative language, no clinical advice, and no reference to the safeguarding concern (draft_01kskez63cz4njpw4drpnpxt42).

**Scheduling Request:** Maria also requested a speech therapy eval for next Tuesday. This is noted but must NOT be acted upon until the safeguarding review is complete. No slots were searched or held.

**Insurance:** No insurance information was provided. This is secondary to the safeguarding concern.

**Language Access:** The caller's name (Maria Gomez) suggests possible Spanish-language preference, but she communicated in English. No Spanish-language response was triggered; however, the clinical lead should be aware in case a bilingual staff member is needed for follow-up.

args.notes: New OT referral received via fax from Dr. Helena Yu at Westside Pediatrics. Child: Owen Brooks, DOB: 2020-02-11. Insurance: Kaiser HMO, Member ID: KSR-4471. Verified as OUT-OF-NETWORK by billing system. Per policy, a benefits conversation with the family must be completed before any slot is held or scheduling proceeds. Parent contact: Rachel Brooks, 555-0103, rachel.brooks@example.com. Family prefers morning slots. Please initiate OON benefits discussion promptly.
args.title: OON Benefits Review Required – Owen Brooks (Kaiser HMO)

args.body:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy, and thank you to Dr. Helena Yu at Westside Pediatrics for referring Owen to us. We're glad you've taken this step and look forward to supporting your family.

We have received the referral for an Occupational Therapy evaluation for Owen. Before we can confirm scheduling, our billing team needs to connect with you to review Owen's out-of-network benefits under his Kaiser HMO plan. This is a required step to ensure you have a clear picture of any potential costs before we proceed.

A member of our billing team will be reaching out to you at 555-0103 or via this email within the next 1–2 business days to walk you through this conversation.

We have also noted that your family prefers morning appointments, and we will do our best to accommodate that preference once the benefits review is complete.

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to contact us.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy Intake Team

items.recommended_next_action: Billing team must initiate an out-of-network benefits conversation with Rachel Brooks (555-0103 / rachel.brooks@example.com) before any slot is held or scheduling is confirmed. Task created (task_01kskf026xcqrddybj7h30ktvj). Once OON benefits are reviewed and family consents to proceed, intake should find morning OT slots and hold one for Owen.

args.title: New PT Referral – Mateo Ramirez (Possible Existing Patient)

args.body:
Hi Carla,

Thank you so much for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy on behalf of Mateo! We're happy to help and have received your request for a Physical Therapy evaluation for toe walking and frequent tripping.

Here's what happens next:
1. Our intake team will review Mateo's referral and verify all details, including insurance and prior authorization requirements with Aetna.
2. A team member will reach out to you at 555-0104 within 1–2 business days to confirm next steps and discuss scheduling options.

In the meantime, if you have a copy of the pediatrician's referral that hasn't been sent yet, please feel free to reply to this email or upload it through our patient portal.

If you have any questions, don't hesitate to reach out. We look forward to working with Mateo!

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy Intake Team

items.recommended_next_action: 1. RESOLVE GUARDIAN DISCREPANCY: A record for "Mateo Ramirez Jr." (DOB 2019-03-15) already exists in the system with guardian "Sofia Ramirez." Intake must call Carla Mendez at 555-0104 to confirm her relationship to Mateo and verify authorization before proceeding. 2. PRIOR AUTH: Aetna requires prior authorization — billing must initiate this before any slot is confirmed. 3. REFERRAL RECEIPT: Confirm the pediatrician's referral has been received in the system. 4. SCHEDULING: Two PT eval slots are available with Priya Shah, PT, DPT — Apr 29 @ 1:00 PM (slot_pt_priya_2026_04_29_1300) and May 6 @ 9:00 AM (slot_pt_priya_2026_05_06_0900). Hold a slot only after guardian identity is confirmed and prior auth is initiated.

items.decision_rationale:
**Classification:** new_referral — Parent is submitting a new PT evaluation request with a pediatrician referral, insurance details, and clinical concern (toe walking/tripping).

**Urgency: P2 (Standard)** — This is a routine new referral with no same-day scheduling urgency, no safeguarding concerns, and no missing critical demographics. Standard 1–2 business day intake follow-up is appropriate.

**Patient Search:** A record for "Mateo Ramirez Jr." (pat_mateo_ramirez_jr, DOB 2019-03-15) was found, but the guardian on file is "Sofia Ramirez," not "Carla Mendez." This discrepancy must be resolved before treating this as an existing patient record. Intake must verify Carla's relationship and authorization.

**Insurance:** Aetna PPO (member ID AET-9910) was verified as in-network and active with a $30 copay. Importantly, prior authorization is required — billing must initiate this before any appointment is confirmed. No OON benefits conversation is needed.

**Service Line:** PT is a supported discipline. Mateo is age 7, within the 0–18 service range. Concern (toe walking, frequent tripping) is appropriate for PT evaluation.

**Slot Search:** Two PT evaluation slots found with Priya Shah, PT, DPT. No slot was held because (a) the parent stated no timing preference, and (b) the guardian identity discrepancy must be resolved first.

**No Escalation Required:** No safeguarding indicators, no same-day cancellation — standard P2 intake workflow applies.

**Task Created:** Assigned to intake (task_01kskf1d3rxsfscey6j0jjn61t) with full context including the guardian discrepancy flag, prior auth requirement, and available slots.

**Draft Message:** Empathetic acknowledgment sent to Carla Mendez at carla.mendez@example.com confirming receipt and setting expectations for 1–2 business day follow-up. No clinical advice provided.

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Difficulty producing R sounds at age 4

args.notes: Parent Jordan Kim submitted a portal message asking whether it is normal for a 4-year-old to not produce R sounds. Per policy, clinical advice cannot be provided over message. Route to SLP screening or evaluation. No insurance info provided yet — collect payer/member ID during intake. Patient not found in database; treat as new inquiry. Draft reply has been sent via portal suggesting a speech screening.
args.title: New SLP inquiry – Ava Kim (age 4, R-sound concern)

args.body:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you so much for reaching out about Ava — it's wonderful that you're paying close attention to her development!

While we truly appreciate your question, our team isn't able to provide clinical guidance or developmental advice through portal messages. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear, personalized picture of where Ava is at is through a professional speech-language screening or evaluation with one of our therapists.

A screening is a great low-commitment first step — it gives our Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) the opportunity to observe Ava directly and share their professional observations with you.

If you'd like to move forward, we'd love to help you get scheduled! Please reply here or give us a call, and our intake team will walk you through the next steps, including any insurance information we may need.

We look forward to supporting Ava and your family!

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

items.recommended_next_action: Intake team should follow up with Jordan Kim via portal to: (1) explain that clinical questions require a professional screening/evaluation, (2) invite the family to schedule an SLP screening for Ava, and (3) collect insurance/payer details and a full date of birth to complete the intake process. No slot should be held until insurance is verified and the family confirms interest in scheduling.

items.decision_rationale:
**Classification: clinical_question**
Jordan Kim is asking whether it is developmentally normal for a 4-year-old to not produce R sounds — this is a direct clinical/developmental question. Per Cedar Kids Therapy policy, clinical advice must not be provided over message and must be routed to a screening or evaluation.

**Urgency: P3**
This is a non-urgent informational inquiry. There is no safeguarding concern, no same-day scheduling issue, and no active patient in crisis. The parent has explicitly stated they are not yet ready to book ("before booking anything"), so no slot search or hold is warranted at this time.

**Patient Search:** No record found for "Ava Kim" — treated as a new inquiry.

**Insurance:** No payer or member ID was provided. Insurance details must be collected during intake before any scheduling proceeds.

**Policy Applied:**
- clinical_advice: Systems must not provide clinical advice over message; routed to SLP screening/eval.
- service_lines: Ava (age 4) falls within the 0–18 age range; SLP is an offered discipline.

**Actions Taken:**
- Created a P2 intake task for the intake team to follow up and invite the family to schedule an SLP screening.
- Drafted a warm, policy-compliant portal reply that declines to give clinical advice, explains the value of a screening, and invites the family to take the next step.

args.title: Incomplete Referral – Sam Taylor (Dr. Omar Keene / Lakeview Pediatrics)

args.body:
Dear Lakeview Pediatrics Team,

Thank you for sending the referral for Sam Taylor from Dr. Omar Keene. We appreciate your trust in Cedar Kids Therapy and are glad to support your patient's care.

Unfortunately, we are unable to process this referral as submitted because several required fields are missing. To move forward with scheduling a Speech-Language Pathology evaluation, we kindly ask that you resend the referral with the following information completed:

1. Child's Date of Birth
2. Parent/Guardian Name and Contact Information (phone number and/or email)
3. Insurance Payer Name
4. Insurance Member ID

Once we receive the completed referral, our intake team will follow up with the family promptly to get Sam scheduled.

Please fax the updated referral to our office at your earliest convenience. If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy – Intake Team

items.recommended_next_action: Intake team should contact Lakeview Pediatrics (Dr. Omar Keene) via fax to request the four missing fields: child's DOB, parent/guardian name and contact, insurance payer, and member ID. A draft fax reply has been prepared (draft_id: draft_01kskf3g3096f06rfnddejb5c1). Once the complete referral is received, verify insurance via billing system — if in-network, proceed to scheduling; if out-of-network, route to billing for a benefits conversation before holding any slot.

args.title: New SLP Referral – Isabella Lopez (5 y/o) – Spanish-Speaking Family

args.body:
Hola, Sra. Lopez. Le llamamos de Cedar Kids Therapy en respuesta a su mensaje de voz sobre una evaluación de habla para Isabella. Nos da mucho gusto poder ayudarle.

Hemos verificado la cobertura de Medicaid de Isabella y todo está en orden. Contamos con una terapeuta de habla bilingüe (español/inglés), Lucia Morales, quien tiene disponibilidad próximamente para realizar la evaluación.

Para confirmar la cita, necesitamos comunicarnos con usted y también recopilar algunos datos adicionales, como la fecha de nacimiento de Isabella. Por favor, devuélvanos la llamada al número de la clínica o espere nuestra llamada en breve.

¡Gracias por contactarnos y esperamos poder apoyar a Isabella muy pronto!

items.recommended_next_action: Intake staff should call Ana Lopez at 555-0107 in Spanish within 1 business day. Confirm Isabella's date of birth, offer the two available bilingual SLP evaluation slots with Lucia Morales (Thu 4/30 at 11:00 AM or Mon 5/4 at 2:00 PM), and complete intake paperwork. Insurance is verified active in-network — no prior auth needed, $0 copay.

items.decision_rationale:
**Classification – New Referral (P2):**
Ana Lopez left a voicemail requesting a speech-language evaluation for her 5-year-old daughter Isabella. No existing patient record was found in the database, confirming this is a new referral. The child is within the 0–18 service range, and SLP is an offered discipline — both eligibility criteria are met.

**Insurance – In-Network, Clean:**
Medicaid was verified as active in-network via the billing system (MCD-55320). No prior authorization is required and the copay is $0. There are no discrepancies between stated and verified payer information. The OON benefits conversation requirement does not apply.

**Language Access:**
Ana explicitly requested a Spanish-speaking provider. Per language access policy, the family must be matched with a bilingual provider. Two SLP evaluation slots are available with Lucia Morales, MA CCC-SLP, who is bilingual (English/Spanish): Thu 4/30 at 11:00 AM and Mon 5/4 at 2:00 PM.

**Slot Hold Decision:**
Ana did not specify a preferred time or day in her voicemail. Per scheduling policy, a slot hold is only placed when the parent explicitly requests a time that matches an available slot. No hold was placed; intake staff will offer both options during the callback.

**Missing Information:**
Isabella's exact date of birth was not provided — only her age (5 years). This must be collected during the intake call.

**Task & Draft:**
An intake task was created (due 2026-04-29) with full context for the bilingual callback. args.title: P1: Same-Day OT Cancellation – Noah Patel (3:00 PM Today)

args.body:
Hi Anita,

Thank you for letting us know so promptly — we're sorry to hear Noah isn't feeling well, and we hope he feels better very soon!

We've received your request to cancel today's 3:00 PM OT appointment and have flagged it for our front desk team to handle right away. A team member will be in touch shortly at 555-0108 or at this email address to help you find a new appointment time that works for your family.

If you have a preferred day or time in mind for rescheduling, feel free to reply here or give us a call — we're happy to accommodate your schedule.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

items.recommended_next_action: Front desk must immediately release Noah Patel's 3:00 PM OT slot for today (2026-04-28) and contact Anita Patel (555-0108 or anita.patel@example.com) to reschedule. One OT slot is available with James Owens, OTR/L on 2026-05-05 at 10:00 AM (slot_ot_james_2026_05_05_1000) — offer to family. No hold placed as parent did not specify a preferred reschedule time.

items.decision_rationale:
**Classification: Scheduling (P1 — Same-Day Cancellation)**

1. **Patient Search:** Noah Patel (DOB: 2017-11-02) was found as an active existing patient (pat_noah_patel), with Anita Patel confirmed as guardian. No discrepancies.

2. **Policy Lookup:** Scheduling policy confirms same-day cancellations/reschedules are P1 operational issues requiring immediate escalation and front desk action.

3. **Slot Search:** One OT slot was found (James Owens, OTR/L — 2026-05-05 at 10:00 AM). However, the parent did not specify any preferred reschedule time, so per policy, no hold was placed. The slot details were passed to the front desk task for them to offer proactively.

4. **Escalation:** P1 escalation filed (esc_01kskf5t9321pvrhbjmynpezy2) due to same-day cancellation requiring immediate operational response.

5. **Task Created:** Front desk task (task_01kskf5t94wx7fm4n8xgmsbt39) created with same-day due date, full context, and actionable instructions including the available slot and parent contact details.

6. **Draft Reply:** Empathetic, professional email drafted to Anita Patel acknowledging the cancellation, confirming front desk follow-up, and inviting her to share timing preferences. No clinical advice provided.

7. **Insurance:** No insurance details were mentioned in this message; not applicable for this cancellation request.
